source: RedClaw R&D Excellence (RDE) Playbook v3.2 — Turbospeed Programme

levers.name: Pre-qualified site network activation
levers.description: Activate pre-vetted sites from the RedClaw Global Site Network (RGSN) with pre-negotiated contracts and pre-qualified investigators. Eliminates 3-5 weeks of site selection + contracting.

levers.name: Risk-based site monitoring (RBSM) — 40% on-site visit reduction
levers.description: Replace protocol-mandated 100% SDV with centralised remote monitoring + targeted on-site visits for critical data only. Reduces site burden and accelerates database lock.

levers.name: Decentralised trial (DCT) elements — remote dosing + ePRO
levers.description: Enable home dosing for subcutaneous formulations and electronic patient-reported outcomes (ePRO) to expand site reach without physical infrastructure. Particularly high impact for rare diseases with sparse patient geography.

levers.name: Adaptive seamless Phase I/II design
levers.description: Combine dose-escalation (Phase I) and dose-expansion/efficacy (Phase II) into a single adaptive protocol with pre-specified interim decision rules. Eliminates 6-10 weeks of protocol amendment + IND update cycle between phases.

levers.name: Master protocol / platform trial basket integration
levers.description: Enrol the asset into an existing RedClaw master protocol (e.g., MORPHEUS, TAPISTRY) to leverage pre-approved regulatory filing, operational infrastructure, and active sites. Eliminates standalone protocol development and regulatory review.

levers.name: Electronic data capture (EDC) pre-configuration — Medidata Rave template
levers.description: Use pre-validated RedClaw EDC templates (Medidata Rave) to reduce database build time from 10 weeks to 3 weeks. Templates validated for oncology, immunology, and haematology indications.

levers.name: Centralised CDx pre-screening agreement — FoundationOne / VENTANA
levers.description: Pre-negotiate a centralised companion diagnostic (CDx) screening agreement with FoundationOne CDx or VENTANA PD-L1 to allow prospective patient identification at activated sites. Eliminates 3-6 week per-site lab qualification.

levers.name: Liquid biopsy ctDNA — replace tissue biopsy inclusion criterion
levers.description: Replace mandatory fresh tissue biopsy with validated ctDNA liquid biopsy (RedClaw cobas cfDNA) as primary inclusion criterion. Increases screen-pass rate by ~30% and reduces screening failure window by 3-5 weeks.

levers.name: IHB organoid-on-a-chip toxicity screen — replace 28-day rat study
levers.description: Use IHB validated organoid-on-a-chip hepatotoxicity and cardiotoxicity panels to complement or replace 28-day repeat-dose rat toxicity studies, subject to regulatory agreement. Concordance ≥0.78 with GLP in-vivo studies.

levers.name: Type B pre-IND meeting — 90-day FDA slot pre-booking
levers.description: Pre-book a Type B (pre-IND) meeting with FDA 90 days before planned IND submission to align on Phase I design, primary endpoint, and biomarker strategy. Prevents clock-stop cycles post-IND filing.
levers.source: RedClaw Regulatory Affairs Strategy Guide v2.1

levers.name: Modular QMS (Opulus Standard) — pre-cleared 510(k) SaMD pathway
levers.description: Apply the Opulus™ FDA 510(k)-cleared (March 26, 2026) modular Quality Management System for SaMD companion diagnostics and AI-assisted imaging biomarkers. Pre-validated cybersecurity module (21 CFR Part 11) eliminates 6-10 week compliance gap remediation.
levers.source: Opulus Standard QMS Module v1.0 — FDA 510(k) K260001

levers.name: Breakthrough Therapy Designation (BTD) application
levers.description: Submit BTD application if preliminary clinical evidence shows substantial improvement over available therapy on ≥1 clinically significant endpoint. BTD enables rolling review and intensive FDA guidance, reducing overall review cycle by 8-12 weeks.
levers.source: RedClaw Regulatory Affairs Strategy Guide v2.1

levers.name: Parallel IND-enabling tox + Phase I CMC scale-up
levers.description: Run IND-enabling GLP toxicology studies in parallel with Phase I manufacturing scale-up (instead of sequential). Requires early go/no-go decision on toxicity risk from BioNeMo prediction. Saves 5-8 weeks of sequential dependency.

levers.name: Single-use bioreactor platform — process intensification
levers.description: Switch from stainless steel to single-use bioreactor (RedClaw RedClaw BioFactory) for Phase I material production. Reduces changeover and validation time by 3-5 weeks and enables 2×-3× batch productivity improvement.

levers.name: BioNeMo early molecular go/no-go gate at 6 months
levers.description: Apply NVIDIA BioNeMo binding affinity + toxicity simulation at 6 months post-SoTD as a formal go/no-go gate. Kill compounds with predicted success probability <0.40 before IND-enabling studies begin. Saves 10-14 weeks per killed compound redirected to next candidate.
levers.source: RedClaw NVIDIA AI Factory Partnership — BioNeMo R&D Gate v1.0
